name: Milk Fever (Hypocalcaemia)
description: Metabolic disease caused by low blood calcium (hypocalcaemia). Occurs around calving. Life-threatening if untreated. Most common in high-producing dairy cows at 2nd–5th calving.
vision_markers: unable to stand flat out on side

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: IV calcium borogluconate infusion – vet administered. Oral calcium supplements for prevention.
supportive_care: Position cow in sternal recumbency (on belly, not flat on side)
Do not milk fully for 24-48 hours post-treatment
Provide fresh water and good hay
treatment_info.first_aid: Roll cow onto chest (sternal). Keep warm. Do NOT give water by drench. Call vet URGENTLY.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not give calcium IV without vet – can cause cardiac arrest if given too fast.
